title: Downstream Datasets Make Surprisingly Good Pretraining Corpora

structured.summary: The paper investigates 'self-pretraining,' using downstream datasets for both pretraining and finetuning to evaluate performance gains compared to traditional transformers like ELECTRA and RoBERTa pretrained on large corpora. It finds that self-pretraining can achieve competitive results and sometimes outperforms standard approaches, suggesting that pretraining objectives are more critical than dataset size.

strengths.point: Demonstrates effectiveness across diverse NLP tasks.
strengths.evidence: The study includes experiments with ELECTRA and RoBERTa models on ten different downstream datasets.

strengths.point: Introduces sentence-reordering heuristic for performance gains.
strengths.evidence: The heuristic improves GLUE benchmark scores by aligning sentence order to enhance long-term dependencies.

weaknesses.point: Lacks detailed transparency in methodology regarding hyperparameters.
weaknesses.evidence: The paper does not specify the hyperparameters or configurations used for self-pretraining.

weaknesses.point: Absence of statistical significance testing in evaluation.
weaknesses.evidence: The results section does not mention any form of statistical significance testing applied to the performance comparisons.
The paper lacks specific procedural details, such as exact pretraining scripts or data-processing methods used.

questions.question: How does self-pretraining generalize to tasks outside the tested datasets?
questions.motivation: Understanding the generalizability of the approach is essential for its broader application across NLP tasks.

critique_points.point_001: Lacks detailed transparency in methodology regarding hyperparameters.. The paper does not specify the hyperparameters or configurations used for self-pretraining
critique_points.point_002: Absence of statistical significance testing in evaluation.. The results section does not mention any form of statistical significance testing applied to the performance comparisons
critique_points.point_003: Insufficient procedural detail for reproducibility.. The paper lacks specific procedural details, such as exact pretraining scripts or data-processing methods used
